what:
OpenFn adaptor for SFTP. The adaptor lets OpenFn workflows read from and write to SFTP (SSH File Transfer Protocol).

It exposes 5 operations that you can call from a workflow step, listed in the Operations tab. 2 ready-to-paste snippets are grouped as create (1) and search (1). Vendor homepage: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc4251.
connect:
IETF / SSH protocol supports HTTP Basic auth or ssh-key for API access.

The credential schema asks for host (required), port, username, and password. Auth reference: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc4252.
when:
Reach for this adaptor when your workflow needs to read or write data in SFTP (SSH File Transfer Protocol).

1 trigger is catalogued (0 webhook, 1 polling), including New File. See the Triggers tab for the full list. IETF / SSH protocol doesn't appear to expose webhooks, so plan on polling at a sensible interval.
wire:
A typical wiring pattern is trigger → fetch → transform → write.

Kick the workflow off from a trigger on the Triggers tab (polled on a cron). Then chain operations from this adaptor's Operations tab to fetch or write data in IETF / SSH protocol. Pass values between steps through state.data — every operation reads from and writes to job state.

To move data between systems, follow this step with another adaptor (e.g. write the fetched records into a downstream FHIR, DHIS2, or database adaptor).
scale:
When running IETF / SSH protocol jobs at volume:

- IETF / SSH protocol doesn't surface webhooks, so poll on the longest interval your SLA allows and use a since-cursor to avoid re-reading everything.
- Batch requests where the API supports it and avoid fan-out loops over large collections.
- Make jobs idempotent by keying on stable external IDs so a replay doesn't create duplicates.
- Surface 4xx/5xx responses with the vendor request ID in your logs, and treat 429s as retries with backoff.

Adapter note: Generic file-transfer protocol adapter — no vendor API. Surface is the SFTP subsystem of SSH; canonical reference is the IETF secsh-filexfer drafts plus the ssh2-sftp-client driver.
best:
- Store IETF / SSH protocol credentials in OpenFn's credential store; never inline keys or tokens in job code.
- Pin @openfn/language-sftp to v3.0.12 in package.json so an upstream change doesn't break a production workflow silently.
- Keep each step small — one logical API call per operation makes failures easier to diagnose and retry.
- Add defensive checks before transforming inbound payloads — vendor responses change between versions.
- Log enough context (resource id, run id, vendor request id) so an incident can be traced end-to-end.
